serverInfo.name: sap.mcp/pipeline_engine_cloud_edition
serverInfo.title: pipeline_engine_cloud_edition
serverInfo.description: MCP server for the pipeline_engine_cloud_edition service.

tools.name: getVersion
tools.title: getVersion
tools.description: Get the version of the vFlow runtime.

tools.name: listGraphs
tools.title: listGraphs
tools.description: This operation can be used to list all graphs from the repository.

parameters.name: regex
parameters.description: Regular expression to be used for finding graphs.

tools.name: getGraph
tools.title: getGraph
tools.description: Find a graph in the repository given its name.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the graph to return.

tools.name: listExecutionGraphs
tools.title: listExecutionGraphs
tools.description: Lists all graphs of the runtime, independent of their state and execution contexts.

tools.name: startExecutionGraph
tools.title: startExecutionGraph
tools.description: Start a specific graph given by a graph execution request.

tools.name: deleteAllExecutionGraphs
tools.title: deleteAllExecutionGraphs
tools.description: Delete or stop all or selected graphs. The `handles` parameter can be used to restrict the operation to a subset of graphs to be stopped or deleted.

parameters.name: stop
parameters.description: If set, only stop the graphs. If not set, delete the graphs.

parameters.name: handles
parameters.description: A comma-separated list of graph handles to restrict the operation.

parameters.name: skipdead
parameters.description: If set, only delete the completed graphs, keeping the dead ones.

parameters.name: includeSnapshot
parameters.description: If set, graphs with snapshot enabled are also deleted.

tools.name: getExecutionGraph
tools.title: getExecutionGraph
tools.description: Get the execution details of a specific graph. This includes information such as the execution status, start time, container details and more.

tools.name: deleteExecutionGraph
tools.title: deleteExecutionGraph
tools.description: Delete or stop a specific graph given by the `handle` parameter.

parameters.name: stop
parameters.description: If set, only stop the graph. If not set, delete the graph.

parameters.name: force
parameters.description: Valid only in conjunction with the `stop` parameter. If set, force graph stopping, i.e., delete the graph pods.

tools.name: POST_v1_runtime_restartgroups_restartGroupID
tools.title: POST_v1_runtime_restartgroups_restartGroupID
tools.description: A restart group can be paused or resumed using this operation.

parameters.name: restartGroupID
parameters.description: The id of the restart group.

tools.name: DELETE_v1_runtime_restartgroups_restartGroupID
tools.title: DELETE_v1_runtime_restartgroups_restartGroupID
tools.description: Use this operation to remove all graphs in restart group

parameters.name: restartGroupID
parameters.description: The id of the restart group.

tools.name: getGraphDescription
tools.title: getGraphDescription
tools.description: Get the graph description of a handle. This operation can be used to obtain design-time information about running graphs.

tools.name: queryExecutionGraphs
tools.title: queryExecutionGraphs
tools.description: Query the list of graph execution objects using filter, sort, limit, and details clauses. Although issued via an `HTTP POST` request, this operation does not change the existing graph execution objects.
